plugin_desc: Pica8 PicOS white-box switch-specific SNMP collection (PICA-PRIVATE-MIB, IANA PEN 35098). PicOS exposes public IF-MIB plus its private MIB only (it does not use NET-SNMP UCD or HOST-RESOURCES MIBs). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects the Pica8 private control-plane CPU utilization (cpuUsage), a clean integer percentage scalar. Memory (totalPhyMemory/usedPhyMemory/freePhyMemory) and temperature (switchTemperature) are exposed only as DisplayString text rather than numeric gauges, fan health is exposed as a speed string plus traps with no pollable fan-state enum, and the redundant power-supply table (rpsustatusTable) has no publicly documented status enum, so memory, temperature, fan and power-supply state are not collected (N/A) to avoid reporting unparseable or unverified values. Supported brands/models — Pica8 white-box switches running PicOS (PICA8-series and partner ODM hardware).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Pica8 PicOS device control plane (PICA-PRIVATE-MIB cpuUsage scalar), reported directly as an integer percentage from 0 to 100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
